---
title: "Introduction to RMarkdown"
author: "Stephanie Garcia"
date: "2026-03-31"
output:
  html_document: default
---

# Introduction

Introduction to RMarkdown.

## Subheading 1

This time tomorrow

## Subheading 2

### Subheading 2.1

### Subheading 2.2

# Create Unordered List

-   List 1
-   List 2
-   List 3

# Create an Ordered List

1.  List 1
2.  List 2
3.  List 3

# R for Reproducible Data Science

# Links

The link to the course material is [here](https://swcarpentry.github.io/r-novice-gapminder/14-knitr-markdown.html)

# Images

## From a website: 
![Stillness](https://www.psdstack.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/08/copyright-free-images-750x420.jpg)

## From my own directory: ERRORS HERE

![Life Expectancy Boxplot]("C:\Users\sjagarci\OneDrive - Florida International University\FIU-STATCONSULT\TRAINING\Professional Development\R-for-Reproducible-Research\R_Reproducible_Research\results\lifeExp_boxplot.png")

# Adding a code and Results of the Code (shortcut: ctrl + alt + i)

## Components of code chunks: 
1. define the language and options in {language, options}

```{r, LoadLibraries}
library(tidyverse)

```

Loading Data for demonstration purposes. 

```{r, LoadData}
gapminder_data <- read_csv("C:/Users/sjagarci/OneDrive - Florida International University/FIU-STATCONSULT/TRAINING/Professional Development/R-for-Reproducible-Research/R_Reproducible_Research/data/gapminder_data.csv")
head(gapminder_data)
```

Loading a graph created in previous class discussions. More specifically, 
Challenge 4: Modify points to different shapes and color by continent with trendline. 
Use color argument inside aes (since this maps to variables). 

```{r}
ggplot(data = gapminder_data) + 
  aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, colour = continent) +
  geom_point(shape = 18) + scale_x_log10() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", linewidth = 1.5)
```
